verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (TMP21/p24delta1) is a type I transmembrane protein whose dominant, steady-state localization is the ER, ERGIC, and Golgi cisternae, where it cycles as a COPI/COPII cargo receptor. Its short cytoplasmic tail contains a canonical COP-binding motif that drives ER/Golgi retention and retrieval. Although its lumenal domain can partially override ER retention and a minor PM pool has been annotated, this is an incidental transit fraction — not a stable outer-leaflet residence. There are no surface-directed therapeutics (ADC, CAR-T, antibody) targeting its ectodomain, and the biology of TMED10 is entirely endomembrane-centric. The protein body is not stably accessible from the extracellular face.
